problems.title: StudyBuddy AI Homework Planner
problems.projectBrief: Schools increasingly assign homework across multiple digital platforms, making it difficult for students to keep track of tasks and deadlines. StudyBuddy solves this by creating an AI-powered planner that automatically collects assignments from different systems and organises them into a personalised study schedule. The app also provides reminders, progress tracking, and accessibility features for students with different learning needs. The goal is to launch a minimum viable product (MVP) before the new school term begins.

stages.title: Stage 1: Market Research
stages.description: You must decide how to validate the idea with students.

choices.brief: Gather direct feedback from real students and teachers to ensure the planner solves actual problems before a single line of code is written.

choices.text: Skip research and start building immediately
choices.brief: Jump straight into development using assumptions, risking building features students don't need.

stages.title: Stage 2: Core System Development
stages.description: The team begins building the core scheduling engine.

choices.brief: Use machine learning to automatically generate personalised study plans, maximising relevance and academic impact.
Create a straightforward drag-and-drop planner students control themselves, keeping costs low and delivery fast.

stages.title: Stage 3: User Experience Design
stages.description: The UX team works on improving usability.

choices.text: Inclusive design testing with diverse students
choices.brief: Run usability sessions across different ability levels and backgrounds to make the app genuinely accessible to all learners.
Ship a functional but untested interface, prioritising speed over polish and accessibility.

stages.title: Stage 4: Unexpected Issue: Accessibility Complaint
stages.description: During testing, visually impaired students report difficulty navigating the interface.

choices.brief: Overhaul navigation and screen-reader support to meet full WCAG standards, turning a complaint into a competitive strength.
Make targeted fixes to the most reported issues, buying time without committing to a full redesign.

stages.title: Stage 5: Security & Privacy Review
stages.description: Your app stores personal student data, raising privacy concerns.

choices.brief: Bring in external experts to audit the app's data practices and close any vulnerabilities before launch.
Postpone the security review to meet the deadline, leaving student data potentially exposed.

stages.title: Stage 6: Marketing Campaign
stages.description: You must promote the app before launch.

choices.brief: Run targeted ads and influencer posts on platforms students already use, driving rapid awareness before the school term.
Work with school IT leads and teachers to embed the app into existing workflows, building organic and lasting adoption.
Ship the app on the planned date to capitalise on back-to-school momentum, accepting any remaining rough edges.
Push the release back to resolve remaining issues, trading perfect timing for a more polished first impression.

problems.title: SafeChat Gaming Moderation Tool
problems.projectBrief: Online gaming communities often suffer from harassment and toxic behaviour, making them unsafe for younger players. SafeChat is a moderation platform designed to detect harmful messages in real time using machine learning. The tool is offered to game developers as an API that automatically scans messages, flags harassment, and protects players. The goal is to release the first version before a major gaming convention where several studios will evaluate the product.

stages.title: Stage 1: Dataset Collection
stages.description: You must collect training data for the AI moderation system.

choices.text: Collect diverse datasets from multiple gaming communities
choices.brief: Source training data from a wide range of game genres and player demographics to build a model that works fairly across all communities.
Train on a limited internal sample to move fast, accepting higher risk of bias and reduced accuracy across different player groups.
Build a context-aware neural model that understands slang, intent, and escalation patterns for deep-level harm detection.
Flag messages containing a predefined list of banned words — fast to build but easy for bad actors to bypass with slight variations.

stages.title: Stage 3: Platform Integration
stages.description: You must integrate the system with gaming platforms.

choices.brief: Deliver a robust, well-documented API that game studios can embed directly into their existing chat infrastructure.
Provide a simplified webhook covering core use cases, reducing development time but limiting compatibility with complex platforms.

stages.title: Stage 4: Crisis: AI Bias Issue
stages.description: The system incorrectly flags messages from certain dialects.

choices.brief: Audit the training data, remove biased samples, and rebuild the model with a more representative and balanced dataset.
Leave the bias in place and continue the rollout, hoping the problem goes unnoticed — at serious risk to trust and fairness.
Deploy encrypted, isolated servers with DDoS protection and regular penetration testing to safeguard all player data.
Use standard cloud hosting with default security settings, keeping costs down but leaving the platform more exposed to attacks.

stages.title: Stage 6: Industry Partnerships
stages.description: Choose who to team up with.

choices.brief: Sign an exclusive deal with a leading studio to integrate SafeChat at scale, creating a high-profile case study for the convention.
Sign multiple agreements with indie studios to build a diverse portfolio of live deployments before the convention.

stages.title: Stage 7: Final Launch
stages.description: Decide on the scale of release.

choices.brief: Release the API publicly worldwide, maximising reach and signalling full confidence in the product's readiness.
Open access to a select group of studios for structured testing, gathering real-world feedback before committing to a full release.
